title: No Taxpayer Funding for the U.N. Population Fund
short_title: No Taxpayer Funding for the U.N. Population Fund
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on Foreign Affairs.
plain_summary: This bill would stop the US government from giving any money to the United Nations Population Fund, which is the UN agency that deals with sexual and reproductive health issues. The government would not be allowed to give money to this organization directly or through other groups. This means no taxpayer money would go to support this UN agency's work. The bill applies to all government departments and agencies.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Foreign Affairs.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the No Taxpayer Funding for the U.N. Population Fund Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Prohibition on United States Contributions to the United Nations Population Fund
sections.text: The United States government cannot use any money to give contributions to the United Nations Population Fund. This includes money from the Department of State and any other government department or agency. The government cannot give money directly to this organization or indirectly through other groups.
